rationale: Both candidates catch the key latent gap (the diffusion transformer's leftover get_cache_scale call), satisfying gap_hit for each. X frames it correctly as a blocker with a concrete grep-verified fix path, and independently hits the ground truth's '+1 keep the regression test' point by arguing forcefully against removing it. Y's technical analysis is impressively deep (actual upstream vLLM source citations) and sharply nails the GT's test-adequacy concern (mapped-name test bypasses the real AutoWeightsLoader mapping it's meant to verify), but it buries its own diffusion-transformer catch as 'non-blocking' under a clean APPROVE verdict and tacks on an unverifiable, likely-fabricated claim that the file already carries a '(see #4810)' comment — which contradicts the gap note's implication that a separate later issue (#4891) was needed, hurting precision.
